questions: Should 'Generate Video' auto-navigate to the video travel tool (ShotEditor) after creating the shot, or should the VideoGenerationModal open inline in the gallery? Opening inline is simpler but the modal may expect to be in the travel tool context.
For timeline clips without a generationId (e.g. text overlays, effects), should the context menu still appear but with disabled items, or should it be hidden entirely?
Is there a preferred naming convention for new shots created from selection (e.g. 'Shot from 3 items', 'Untitled Shot', or should a naming modal appear)?
Should the 'Create Shot' action add items as positioned (with timeline_frame) or unpositioned? Unpositioned is simpler and lets users arrange later.

For the button-based alternative (Step 8), is a floating action bar the right pattern, or should these be added to an existing toolbar/header? Is there an existing selection-dependent UI I should extend?

success_criteria.criterion: Right-clicking a selected gallery item shows a context menu with 'Create Shot' and 'Generate Video' options
'Create Shot' creates a new shot containing all currently selected items (single or multi-select)
'Generate Video' creates a new shot from selected items and opens VideoGenerationModal with that shot
Context menu appears correctly for both single-select and multi-select scenarios
Right-clicking an unselected item selects it first, then shows the context menu
Context menu dismisses on click-away, Escape key, and after completing an action
No regressions to existing gallery selection, lasso selection, drag-and-drop, or timeline context menu
Timeline clip context menu includes Create Shot and Generate Video options for clips with generation assets
Button-based alternatives exist for Create Shot and Generate Video when items are selected (not only right-click)
Context menu uses existing design tokens (bg-popover, text-popover-foreground, shadow-md) and matches app visual style
Batch shot creation handles 10+ items without UI freezing (optimistic updates or loading state)
New component files stay under ~300 lines each
Manual smoke tests pass for all 5 flows listed in Step 9

assumptions: VideoGenerationModal can be rendered outside the video travel tool's component tree — it only needs a Shot object, not ShotEditor context. Will verify during implementation.
No batch insert RPC exists for shot_generations; we'll use Promise.all over the single-image RPC. If performance is an issue for large selections, a batch RPC can be added later.
Items will be added to new shots as unpositioned (timeline_frame = null) since the user hasn't arranged them yet. They can position them in the shot editor afterward.
Auto-generated shot names like 'Shot from N items' are acceptable as a default; no naming modal will be shown (keeping the flow fast). Users can rename later.

The gallery context menu and timeline context menu are independent — they read from their respective selection systems (GallerySelectionContext vs useMultiSelect). 'Across both' in the spec means both UIs support the feature, not that selections merge.
The portal-based context menu pattern from ClipAction.tsx is the preferred approach (no need to install a new shadcn primitive).
